title: The KdV conservative flux of a constant state is nonlinear times c squared

source.notes: Constant-state simplification for the configurable KdV-like conservative flux.

informal.statement: For a constant state u_i=c, the KdV-like flux nonlinear*u_i^2 + dispersion*secondDifference(u)_i reduces to nonlinear*c^2.
informal.latex: If \(u_i=c\), then \(a u_i^2 + b\Delta^2u_i = a c^2\).
assumptions: nonlinear, dispersion, and c are real numbers
i is a natural-number grid index
the second forward difference of a constant state is zero
informal.expected_conclusion: The dispersion contribution vanishes for a constant state.

declarations.name: kdvConservativeFlux
declarations.statement: def kdvConservativeFlux (nonlinear dispersion : Real) (u : Nat -> Real) (i : Nat) : Real := nonlinear * (u i) ^ 2 + dispersion * secondForwardDifference u i

declarations.name: kdvConservativeFlux_const
declarations.statement: theorem kdvConservativeFlux_const (nonlinear dispersion c : Real) (i : Nat) : kdvConservativeFlux nonlinear dispersion (fun _ => c) i = nonlinear * c ^ 2

tasks.input: Translate the constant-state simplification of the KdV conservative flux into Lean 4.
tasks.target: theorem kdvConservativeFlux_const (nonlinear dispersion c : Real) (i : Nat) : kdvConservativeFlux nonlinear dispersion (fun _ => c) i = nonlinear * c ^ 2

tasks.input: Given kdvConservativeFlux and secondForwardDifference, complete the constant-state proof.
tasks.target: A proof by unfolding definitions and using ring.
